title: oberna behen var. carpatica
intro: Oberna behen var. carpatica, also known as the Common Bugle or Wood Bugle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Oberna behen var. carpatica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried leaves and roots are also used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
